reviews.review_description:
This game is still clearly in its developmental stages, but it is very good in its current state and yet still has room for potential. The premise seems bizarre - if not downright silly - but somehow it works. There's just something so addictive about it. The flight mechanics, freedom of movement and universe scale are the keys to playability, as well as the variation in missions and gameplay. The only thing this game needs is more content. Even with a meaty recent update, it's still a fairly short game. This hidden gem fully deserves its sky-high ratings, but I hope the devs give us more.

l i n k t r . e e
/
q u a c k b i r t h

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for checking out the game! We're very proud of the flight system and movement in Space Dragon. It's a tricky thing to get right without potentially making someone sick. Glad you enjoyed it. We have plans for more content down the line and we added a dragon shooter to our mixed reality party game, Grokit, as well.

reviews.review_description:
I really like this! The open space gives so nice freedom feeling, the dragons are cute, and the action is fun! I'd recommend this:

- Include more ambient sounds, like dragon noises and roars, digitised voices  etc so you feel more immersed in the game.
- You could use one of the pad buttons to create a temporary shield for each dragon, and the other one to charge a super devastating beam, that would be superb. 
- Would be GREAT to fly over frozen, stormy or lava planets, entering caves and exploring them a bit. That exploration of space and action mix would be trully awesome!

Thanks and lot and keep updating this great idea!

Cheers ;-)

developer_response.body: We have a sound update coming soon to add more atmosphere in the game as you suggested. Thank you so much for playing!

reviews.review_description: New to this whole quest 3 thing and I just gotta say the graphics on this game are out of this world!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We love to hear that!

reviews.review_title: More fun than I was expecting
reviews.review_description: The store video didn't fill me with high hopes but I actually enjoyed this game a lot once I played it. There is a bit of a learning curve with movement but once you have the hang of it there is quite some skill in what seems deceptively simple. A variety of styles of game play from rapid shooter to navigating platformer style obstacles. The graphics are simple but work well for the style of the game. The only thing I found a little off, for me at any rate, were the screams of terror from the planet I was required to destroy. I'm all for fancy dragon flying and taking out weapon satellites but not feeling like I'm murdering millions of people.

reviews.review_title: I am become dragon, destroyer of worlds
reviews.review_description:
I love dragons, so that's already a great start :D

I haven't played a ton of games, much less VR ones, but this was a great experience! The movement was intuitive, the gameplay simple but fun, and I enjoyed it so much I finished my first playthrough in one sitting.

Also, it's really pretty! Love the way the dragons and bosses glitter as they move fluidly through space.

developer_response.body: Thank you! Our developers worked so hard to make sure the movement system was intuitive and easy to pick up. Thank you for playing!

reviews.review_description: Space Dragons is an exhilarating and visually stunning gaming experience that immerses players in the captivating role of controlling a pair of hatchling space dragons. The game’s innovative move-and-warp system adds a refreshing twist to traditional gameplay mechanics, making it a standout among space-themed games.  This game is a definite must play especially if you enjoy a unique experience. Great work from the team.

Wow this game has changed so much, I recently jumped back in to play as it’s had quite a few updates. First off it’s a great sci-fi arcade shooter, you fly around with your dragon arms defeating enemies and bosses. Now I could speed run this in 17 minutes on the old version but so much content has been added, I hadn’t even got past the second boss and it had been about 45 minutes. Honestly for under £8 this is certainly a game you need in your library edition if you like cool looking arcade shooters.

developer_response.body: Glad you enjoyed the new updates, Clickster! We definitely wanted to expand and ramp up the difficulty. Thanks for playing again!

reviews.review_description: The graphics appear simple and unassuming, but the gameplay definitely lures you in. I reached the Phoenix (?) battle and got stuck and found myself restarting again and again (it's much appreciated that there are checkpoints mid-battle or I might have rage quit). The movement is very intuitive. I want to compare it to Iron Man or Spider Man as there's definitely some zooming about (zero motion sickness, fortunately) but you always feel in control. It's fun to swoop at your targets from all different angles. Being stuck as I am in the current battle, though, I do worry about the game becoming so hard that I give up (I'll admit, it doesn't take a lot for me, especially with 30 other games on my headset distracting me). I hope there's eventually some kind of progression system, powerups or different weapons to hold my interest. At the same time, I hope it stays simple as I don't get on well with having to use a bunch of buttons. But it's definitely still fun to play in the current state :)

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing! We did ramp up the difficulty a bit on our last update as the common feedback was how easy the game is. For the Phoenix, use the green translucent solar panels for cover so you can stay close to it when the solar flares erupt.

reviews.review_description: I played Space Dragon before the update and after the new update was released.  They added new elements to the game to add to the gameplay. You fly through space as two dragons defeating suns and various monsters while flying through space. There are also tunnels to fly through while avoiding lasers. This game not only looks amazing but is super fun!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing! Those lasers are real jerks and that was the point!

reviews.review_description: This game is a great VR arcade style shooter! Your controllers turn into two dragons that fly around space fighting the creatures that captured them. The flight style is unique in that you thrust your arms forward while holding down the movement button which makes for a unique style of game play where you can move, pivot, and shoot at the same time! The 3LBS dev team has down a great job paying attention to movement in a style that will not easily make people motion sick even if they are not use to VR much. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Thanks, Historian! We went through SO many movement systems, testing them on one of our developers who is prone to motion sick to make sure we got it right. Thank you so much for your support!

reviews.review_title: Fun game play and great visuals!
reviews.review_description: Love the aesthetic and flight controls! It's great to be able to move in any direction at will while shooting, it creates a great rhythm to the game play. The warp space between levels is also super satisfying to traverse. Looking forward to playing more!

developer_response.body: Keep your eye up for an update with more content in the next few months. We're glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: Super fun and intuitive.  Who doesn’t love having g dragons as arms. Great music and visuals!!!  Looking forward to my new fav obsession

developer_response.body:
Our CEO actually has a dragon hand puppet she randomly plays with during team meetings. 

Thanks for playing! We're glad you enjoyed it.

reviews.review_title: This might be my new favorite game!
reviews.review_description: It's a cross between meditative, trippy, and blow-things-up-explosives-fun! These are the kinds of games that Meta needs more of!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing! We're glad you enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description:
Space Dragon is an amazing bullet hell, arcade space shooter, with unique control system. Each of your hands become a space dragon, which you flying and blasting through the cosmos simply by moving your hands around. The game gives me strong retro gaming vibes from the 80s and 90s gaming time. Specially reminds me of Contra during the boos fights.

The game is a very immersive VR game that will get your adrenaline pumping. From the amazing control system, to the dodging bullets and firing back. This is an arcade bullet hell shooter done right in VR.

The gameplay is is a challenge, it is going to keep you moving. You will feel the excitement and heart pumping to this one. Specially the boss fights. Each one a huge unique boss, firing bullets in every direction. Find your opening and make each shot count. These bosses remind me of the boss fights from the NES Contra game. Big, bullets everywhere and targets you got to take down first before exposing the bosses weak point.  

The controls take a few minutes to get use to. After that you will be soaring through the cosmos like a pro, taking down enemies and blasting planets to free your fellow dragons.

The VR world is very immersive and beautiful. It will eat up all your focus. All you will think about is guiding your dragons through bullet hell and taking down the enemy. 

If you love bullet hell arcade style games, in an amazing VR world, this game is it. Specially if you gamed in the 80s and 90s, you will feel that heart pumping arcade shooting action again. Get your dragons and dive in for some fast action VR gaming fun.

Watch my preview here,
https://youtu.be/N0WVc17vtKI

developer_response.body: Ironbug, thank you so much for the review! Love the callbacks to the NES days. That's definitely the vibe we were shooting for (pun intended).

reviews.review_description: Its amazing graphics and experiences. Great Game

As a non-gamer or maybe officially now first time gamer, I embarked on an unforgettable journey into the cosmos with 3lbGames' Space Dragons. This exciting game places you in control of a pair of adorable hatchling space dragons. Your controllers are space dragons, how cool is that. I wanted to protect my hatchling space dragons at all cost.

Destroying the sun, navigating the wormholes was, destroying planets, firing back and dodging enemy fire was exhilarating and epic. Every time I jumped back into headset I discovered something new that I didn't notice the previous gameplays.

Loved the visuals and sound effects. As a first-time gamer, 3lbGames' Space Dragons provided an exhilarating and accessible entry into the gaming world. 

Thank you 3lbGames!!

developer_response.body: Wow! We're honored that we were your entry point into gaming! So glad you enjoy Space Dragon!

reviews.review_description:
Disclaimer: I got this game free from the devs to play and to give an honest review. 
This game is beautiful! Your hands become Dragon’s  that fly through space to fight suns and bosses! The gameplay is fun and the tutorial was well done. I played on the Quest Pro and played both the AR and VR modes. Both were great but I preferred the VR mode as I do in most games! Kudos to the devs for making this uniquely fun and beautiful game!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review. We're glad you enjoyed the game!

reviews.review_title: Bullet Hell in Space?! YES, PLEASE!!!
reviews.review_description:
I did not know what to think when I saw these two dragons breathing ethereal fire from their mouths, and was expecting a simple fly and shoot tartgets game.

Man. Was I wrong. This is one of the best bullet hell games in the Meta Quest store!

The flight mechanics are simple for anyone to pick up and begin playing, but the difficulty level is "R-Type" crazy (which, in my opinion, is a great thing)! Each level presents you with ships and turrets to shoot at, with levels that reminded me of some of the Star Wars Squadron 3D levels, taking your two dragons through mazes of spires and energy cores as you dodge and fight your way to uncovering the major boss battle. The current boss I am on is the Kraken (one of my favorite mythical creatures (and a tasty rum), and it is one of the most difficult bosses to defeat in the game! I have spoken to some of the team members of 3lb Games, and they agree it is one of the toughest boss battles in the game!

However, does this mean I would stop playing because it was too hard? No. This is what bullet hell-style games are all about. You have to work at trying to find the best strategy, and sometimes it takes a multitude of different times and different strategies to run through the level until you find one that works. It is really a fantastic game, and I hope to see a sequel in the future, because we really need more games like this in VR.

Awesome job, 3lb Games!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the detailed review! The original version of the game was called too easy, so we ramped that difficulty up with the lasers and missiles, among other things. Keep cracking on the Kraken. Good luck!

reviews.review_title: BEST GAME I HAVE EVER F@CKING PLAYED
reviews.review_description:
The controls are simple to learn and very unique but allow for a lot of mobility.

The shooting is very satisfying and simple. 

The boss fights are absolutely MAGNIFICENT, beautiful, fun, and honestly pretty epic.

The music is pretty good too.

The story is pretty simple and straightforward but I don’t think any of us are here for the story. 

So far in my playthrough the levels have been kind of easy though. 

And like most cheaper app lab games the frame rate is a tidge low and in my case it gives me headaches after about an hour.

Overall a great game that I HIGHLY recommend, definitely worth the price,

developer_response.body: We're working on an update to add a 'hard mode' difficulty level to the game that will take 'bullet hell' to the next level. Also some changes to the boss fights and a few other things we can't talk about quite yet. The note on the framerate is appreciated. We ported this from Steam and downgraded a LOT, but clearly still have some more work to do. Thank you so much for playing!

reviews.review_description: Single player game but I've had all my friends take a turn. We all love having dragons for arms! Easy to fly around and shoot, but still challenging to complete. Excellent visuals and sound. Looking forward to future updates.
